items.title: Iran war unlikely to trigger terror attacks in Indonesia: ex-militants
Modern economic chokepoints in war and the impact on geopolitics
items.description: Hoover Institution fellow Eyck Freymann explains what the Iran war and the blockade of the Straight of Hormuz reveals about modern economic chokepoints, geopolitics and war strategies.

items.title: Trump casts doubt on Iran peace deal and says Tehran has not ‘paid a big enough price’
items.description: US president signals scepticism to latest Iranian peace proposal but Tehran says it has received a US responseDonald Trump has cast doubt on the prospect of a diplomatic breakthrough by claiming Iran had not yet “paid a big enough price” for its past wrongs.However, Iran’s foreign ministry said on Sunday it had receive…

items.title: Is Israel expanding into Gaza despite ceasefire?
items.description: Redi Tlhabi speaks to Ray Adams Row Farr from Forensic Architecture about Israel’s expansion into Gaza post-ceasefire.

items.title: Can Israel and Lebanon move from war to diplomacy? - Sponsored Content
items.description: Lebanese lawmaker Fouad Makhzoumi, Israeli analyst Sarit Zehavi, and Middle East expert Robert Satloff to unpack what’s at stake--and what’s possible-- in Lebanon. The post Can Israel and Lebanon move from war to diplomacy? appeared first on The Times of Israel.

items.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill three
items.description: Ukraine and Russia exchanged cross-border drone strikes as the war continued. Across Ukraine, at least three people were killed in overnight Russian attacks. Drones struck residential buildings and port infrastructure in the southern city of Odesa, causing significant damage. Strikes were also reported in the frontline…

items.title: Forty Children On Bus In Ukraine Narrowly Escape Russian Drone Strike
items.description: A Russian drone struck close to a bus carrying 40 children in Ukraine’s Dnipropetrovsk region, as the children were heading to a resort in western Ukraine amid continued air attacks between Moscow and Kyiv.

items.title: Russia stands by Mali's military junta at all costs
items.description: Russian Africa Corps mercenaries have suffered several setbacks since jihadists launched an offensive on April 25, including the death of pro-Russian defense minister Sadio Camara and the loss of the town of Kidal.

items.title: 'Netanyahu convoca gabinetto sicurezza su possibile stop alla tregua in Iran e Gaza'
items.description: Lo riportano i media israeliani, riunione attesa per oggi

items.title: Ukraine Hits Russian Oil Sites as 8 Killed in Both Countries
items.description: Ukraine said Sunday it had hit several Russian ships as both sides fired hundreds of drones.

items.title: Putin’s Chornobyl: a Régime That Will Not Permit the Wound to Heal
items.description: From the 2022 Red Forest trenches to the St. Valentine’s 2025 drone strike, the Kremlin’s war is a war on the truth of 1986 – and the men who buried that truth are now being decorated for it.

items.title: How the Iran war is hurting travelers, airline industry
items.description: Europeans may have to reconsider their summer travel plans, as a jet fuel shortage triggered by the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz could lead airlines to set higher ticket prices.

items.title: A war in Europe drags on, and Africans pay the price
items.description: Citizens of Ghana, Kenya, and several other African nations are ending up on the front lines in Ukraine as Russia looks overseas to bolster its armed forces.

items.title: War criminal Mladic close to death, say lawyers asking judge for jail release
items.description: Mladic, 84, was jailed for life for genocide, war crimes and crimes against humanity during the Bosnian war in 1992-95.

items.title: Nuclear fears resurface among younger generations amid global tensions
items.description: The threat of nuclear Armageddon, a constant worry for many who grew up during the tense decades of the Cold War, is becoming a cause for concern for a growing number of young people.
